tree.title: What should I eat tonight?
tree.description: A decision tree to help you figure out dinner.

branch.content: Are you cooking or ordering in?
How much time do you have?
Cooking for yourself or others?
Substantial or light?
Pasta with jarred sauce, or fried eggs on toast with hot sauce. Both are ready in under 10 minutes and barely any washing up.
Greek yoghurt with granola and fruit, or a quick salad with whatever protein is in the fridge. Five minutes and done.
Any dietary restrictions to work around?
Tacos. Use pre-cooked or canned protein, lay out toppings, and let everyone build their own. On the table in 15 minutes.
A grain bowl bar. Cook rice or couscous, lay out toppings. Works for meat-eaters, vegetarians, and gluten-free guests alike.
Do you eat meat?
Hearty, lighter, or noodles?
Pan-seared chicken thighs with roasted vegetables. Season well, high heat, 40 minutes. No real technique required.
Baked salmon with a quick salad. The salmon needs 15 minutes in the oven. Add lemon and capers and it is basically done.
A simple ramen-style broth with a soft-boiled egg and whatever vegetables you have. Surprisingly satisfying for the effort involved.
What sounds good?
Lentil soup or a simple bean stew. Aromatics in, lentils in, simmer 25 minutes. Make extra for tomorrow.
Tofu with vegetables over rice. Press the tofu first for crispiness. Ready in 35 minutes and genuinely satisfying.
Pasta primavera or cacio e pepe. Both are vegetarian, both come together quickly once the pasta is boiling.
Feeling adventurous?
branch.fork: Yes, let us go all out

branch.content: Which cuisine?
A curry from scratch. Toast whole spices, build a proper base, let it simmer low and slow. The smell alone is worth it.
Braised carnitas or chicken enchiladas. Both benefit from a long cook and reward the patience.
Slow-roasted lamb shoulder with herbs and lemon, or a proper moussaka. Effort upfront, then the oven does the work.
Classic comfort or something richer?
Slow-roasted chicken with potatoes and garlic. Put it in at low heat and forget about it for 90 minutes. Classic for a reason.
A proper beef stew or a Bolognese. Both improve with time on the stove and will feed you for two days.
What are you in the mood for?
Comfort or quality?
A big chain is fine for this. Order extra and have leftovers for tomorrow. No shame in it whatsoever.
Find the highest-rated independent pizzeria near you and order their signature pie. Worth the wait and the price.
Which kind?
Check reviews carefully and go for a busy place. Fresh fish matters. Splurge a little if you can.
Dumplings, fried rice, and something saucy. Order more than you think you need. The classics exist for a reason.
Pad Thai or a green curry. Ask for the real spice level, not the tourist version. Have jasmine rice on the side.
Find a dedicated ramen place if you can. The difference between a proper tonkotsu broth and a generic one is enormous.
Delivery or going to pick up?
Burritos travel better than tacos. Order guacamole even if it costs extra. You know you want it.
A proper taqueria beats delivery every time. Find one with handmade tortillas if you can.
Mild or properly spiced?
A butter chicken or korma. Rich, fragrant, comforting. Get a garlic naan without question.
A vindaloo or a lamb rogan josh. Tell them you want it authentic. Have yoghurt ready.
Saag paneer or a dal makhani. Some of the best Indian food is vegetarian. Do not overlook it.
Fast food or a proper burger?
Go for it. Get the fries and a milkshake while you are at it. No judgment.
Find a local spot with good reviews. A smash burger with proper cheese and a brioche bun is hard to beat.
